As a continuation of my Pandia post (which didn't fit due to the character limit), I'd also like to offer some suggestions regarding Casiah, who is another character I'm very passionate about <:Catsiah:1441868724226228265> 

Casiah's current standing in the game is considerably better than Pandia's, and I am very happy about the strength generation buffs she's receiving, but I'd still like to use the 2.5.1 buffs as a potential opportunity to address her passive "Master of Magic". 

It currently increases Casiah's base damage based on her personal Realm Mastery stat whenever she unleashes exalt, which is not a particularly useful effect on a character with low damage and no additional multipliers in her kit. It also doesn't synergize with the temporary strength she generates. Due to these reasons, here are some potential ideas:

- If the intention is to make Casiah into a supportive damage dealer, then perhaps the passive could give her a smaller amount of final damage scaling instead, so that the strength is accounted for.

- Alternatively, it could make her exalt and/or Telekinesis enjoy a bigger strength bonus based on the Realm Mastery she has.

- If Casiah is to remain a pure support, then perhaps her passive could slowly scale up the amount of strength she generates based on Realm Mastery.

- As another idea, it could apply a small amount of temporary strength reduction on enemies based on the strength Casiah generates, with the scaling being impacted by Realm Mastery.

- Or it could simply refund a part of her exalt cost per each point of Realm Mastery.

Once again, thank you for reading through my suggestions!! <:cute1:1356053053568192717>
thread_title: Some passionate feedback on Casiah's 2.5.1 buffs

content:
Pandia's problem:

- Her kit doesn't make sense: -> you're supposed to generate temporary counter with multi hits from 3 costs + strike card, 2 cost remove enemies counter and steal STR temporarily, Rouse generate small amount of permanent counter based on multi hit.

- Looking at her kit, what the fuck is she supposed to do??? The multi hit is only 3 hit from her 3 costs, if it was 6 multi hit from it + 3 multi hit from strike card then it would have been better with Rouse

- First talent buff was just a small buff in the damages dealt and making her 3 costs prepare, SHE WOULDN'T HAVE HAD A PROBLEM WITH DAMAGES IF THE COUNTER REGEN WAS GOOD TO BEGIN WITH.

- Then came Axiom buffs, 3% STR gain based on amount of permanent counter gained, Rouse perma counter is basically shit, only way to get good amount is from image with 20% damages convertion into perma counter, but you can just daffodil to regen 400k temporary counter then nuke with her exalt and her Axiom becomes useless, Rouse is only useful if you're pairing her with pickman but it's just pickman being good and elevating everyone.

- Last and most recent buff, another DMG buffs and counter regen, but it doesn't adress just how awkward it is to play this kit overall.
thread_title: Some passionate feedback on Pandia's 2.5.1 buffs

content:
ADENDUM: Following the incredible feedback other players gave I would like to add another suggestion, that I personally think is very feasible. 

1) I don't think the fixed counter generation is fit for the late game, specially with those low numbers. Keeper of the Lost, a 4 arithmetica command card, gives less than 100 counter. And why does it scales off ATK? Why not make it scale off her DEF stat instead? It would make much more sense and the number would be much better. 

2) Her Exalt also have pretty low numbers, both the shield and the counter are very very low for an exalt of an limited awakener. And again, the counter scales of her ATK, instead of DEF. When compared to the base shield the latest character, Arachne gives, its almost nothing. Arachne gives over 200 shield when Wanda barely gives 100. 

3) The last thing i would like to comment on is the fact that when she consumes 5 Dreamlure she gives temporary counter. When you  consider all the setup that is needed to have the 5 Dreamlure stacks and them Exalt it makes this restriction very devastating for her kit. This may be a bit controversial, but can't she give permanent counter on this effect and the percentage be lower. Something like: 

"Consume 5 stacks of Dreamlure, Wanda obtain X Aliemus and gains counter equal to 30% of permanent counter."

I Don't know if any of this suggestions are too much, but i personally think that she wouldn't be broken with any of this, specially when you consider the other stronger Awakener's in the game. 

Once again, I thank you guys for listening.
Agree, 4 cost like Wanda and Tulu should have prepare and retain.

I enjoy pdive, but the recent speedrun aspect of it not so much

The ideal pdive experience for me would be a layer exploration system: keep delving new maps, new bosses as long as you want during your run, but the longer you make your dive last, the more dangerous and rewarding it is, with score determined not by speed, but by risk overcome

I understand that due to how morimens works,  implementing this in a way that is interesting under the current rules would be a significant challenge, but still throwing the idea out
thread_title: Delve system for pdive (possibly separate game mode)

thread_title: Delve system for pdive (possibly separate game mode)

content:
Madness doesnt really work in HSR as the gap between hard and madness makes it inoperable for a large majority of players, and require rerolls (sign of defect) for many others. As of writing this i am tied at 1500 pts with at least 1000+ players in HSR, because we all maxed out hard but none of us can clear madness.

Therefore i suggest a heat/ascension alternative system for customizable  difficulty increase HSR, rather than raw stat creep

-more curses
-more ennemies
-progressive ennemy scaling
-debuffs
-gimmicks that force adaption
Etc

Either through a heat system (player chooses how many and which debuffs in exchange for score multiplier) or an ascension system (cumulative static debuffs unlocked one at a time)
It's still a very early concept. We will see how it gets worked out. :/
